Work Blog Open Source Papers Contact

Case Study

Lance Event Bridge

Semantic indexing from live event streams into LanceDB without standing up a separate vector database.

Focused prototype Python LanceDB NATS JetStream Embeddings Apache Arrow
Back to workbench ← Peek-a-Boo druid-datafusion-bridge →

Why it exists

There is a recurring gap between live event streams and small-footprint semantic search. Many teams want embeddings over fresh events, but not the operational overhead of a separate vector database cluster.

Technical center

The bridge consumes JetStream batches, normalizes the payloads, computes embeddings with the LanceDB model registry or explicit batch embedding calls, and writes LanceModel rows into a local store that can be queried back as Arrow tables.

Current proof points

The public article already exposes the real engineering surface instead of hand-waving: the schema model, embedding flow, NATS message handler, compaction caveats, zero-copy Arrow reads, and 2 tracked PRs in lance-format/lance that show hands-on familiarity with the underlying storage internals.

Repository Related article Lance upstream
Lance format

The storage side of the bridge: random-access columnar files, native vector indexing, and Arrow-first interoperability.

Event source

The streaming side: Nephtys and JetStream provide the event feed that the bridge turns into a semantically searchable local store.
